Title: A viral protein promotes host SAMS1 activity and ethylene production for the benefit of virus infection

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Methionine/SAM supply module upstream of ACC and ethylene formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts L-methionine and ATP into S-adenosyl-L-methionine (SAM), with production of pyrophosphate and phosphate
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates plant development and biotic stress responses; in this study promotes susceptibility to Rice dwarf virus infection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes SAM, ACC, and ethylene accumulation and thereby enhances rice susceptibility to RDV infection
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; RNAi knockdown; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; metabolite profiling of SAM/ACC/ethylene; in vitro enzyme assay; virus infection assay
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key component of ethylene synthesis pathway; upstream precursor-supplying step
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Assessed in rice seedlings/leaves at 40–42 days and during RDV infection; mRNA/protein not altered by Pns11 overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Pns11 specifically enhances OsSAMS1 activity without changing OsSAMS1 transcript or protein abundance and without altering substrate affinity to L-Met or ATP; developmental defects observed in RNAi/KO lines
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsSAMS1 was identified as the specific rice interactor of RDV Pns11. 2. Pns11 enhances OsSAMS1 enzymatic activity in vitro and in vivo. 3. OsSAMS1 overexpression increases SAM, ACC, and ethylene and enhances RDV accumulation and disease, whereas RNAi/knockout lowers these metabolites and increases tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: RNAi and knockout caused suppressed seed germination, dwarfism, and reduced fertility; grain-size change noted in related transgenics
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing OsSAMS1 activity/expression could improve viral tolerance, but strong loss of function causes developmental penalties; partial modulation or pathway-specific engineering may be preferable
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: RDV infection; ACC treatment; AVG treatment

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Positive signaling transduction in ethylene response pathway
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Ethylene-responsive pathway genes; OsERF3 used as ethylene-response marker
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes ethylene-responsive gene expression and susceptibility-associated downstream responses
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central component of ethylene signaling required for ethylene-promoted susceptibility to RDV
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates development and stress responses; here enhances virus susceptibility when signaling is active
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases susceptibility to RDV, whereas osein2 loss-of-function increases tolerance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis (osein2/mhz7); overexpression; virus infection assay; northern blot; western blot; genetic test of OsSAMS1 overexpression in osein2 background
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Analyzed in overexpression lines and osein2 mutant during RDV infection
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsEIN2-mediated ethylene signaling enhances RDV susceptibility. 2. osein2 mutants show reduced virus accumulation and lower infection rate. 3. Overexpression of OsSAMS1 in osein2 background does not restore high susceptibility, confirming signaling dependence.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Ethylene-insensitive mutants may alter normal ethylene-regulated development, though detailed penalties were not quantified here
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Weakening ethylene signaling via OsEIN2 could improve RDV tolerance, but possible developmental effects of ethylene insensitivity must be managed
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper names MHZ7 as OsEIN2 and treats it as a central ethylene signaling component
